texto: Often in the morning he drove a long hour or more to the markets in the city, there to behold what would determine the day’s special. With the crates of fresh selesctions snuggled into his station wagon, his thoughts on the ride back confronted the culinary equivalent of the writer’s blank page. Sometimes his head swirled with exciting ideas; other mornings he was in a panic upon returning with the same old eggplant and squash and zucchini and nothing but the dullness of the word ratatouille standing by to mock him.

texto: What one gains in technique can lead to deforestation in the writing that is both good and bad. Keep the energy and the willingness to proceed stupidly.
explicacao: Citação de Nancy Zafris sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “What one gains in technique can lead to deforestation in the writing that is both good and bad. K…” articula reflexao, writing-process, gains em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: He pressed the herb to his nose. Thyme. He loved the name and he loved the smell. He looked out the window at the illusion of deep woods. His face too was out there, hung on a tree and returning his gaze. He drew close to the glass to lose the mirror effect. Outside, the forest panted its beefy halitus; the soil held the breaths of gloom in its dampness. Fifteen thousand years ago a glacier had sliced through this park he was living in, bringing with it the nutrients from all its travels. Fifteen thousand years ago human beings were the fable that frightened the dark woods.
explicacao: Citação de Nancy Zafris sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “He pressed the herb to his nose. Thyme. He loved the name and he loved the smell. He looked out t…” articula vida, homecoming, nostalgia em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como viagem e reflexao-noturna.

Outside, the forest panted its beefy halitus; the soil held the breaths of gloom in its dampness. Fifteen thousand years ago a glacier had sliced through this park he was living in, bringing with it the nutrients from all its travels. Fifteen thousand years ago human beings were the fable that frightened the dark woods.
